summary: Green Greens stage decompilation pass centered on Whispy/wind and block-grid behavior. The PR expanded grGreens.c with implementations for the Whispy state machine, block initialization/spawn/fall/collision cleanup helpers, and supporting data tables; updated Ground vars, function prototypes, grmaterial declarations, and GALE01 data symbols. decomp.dev reported two new 100% function matches, grGreens_802139C4 and grGreens_80214674, plus large partial improvements across remaining grgreens functions, with known .sdata/.data regressions.

key_files.role: Main Green Greens implementation; added Whispy/wind logic, block-grid helpers, data tables, random timer behavior, item/audio calls, and matching tweaks.
Added struct grGreens_GroundVars2 and exposed it in Ground.gv as greens2 for the alternate Green Greens actor state.
Replaced UNK prototypes with typed signatures for grGreens_802150C4, grGreens_80215D54, and grGreens_80215ED8.
Moved/added grMaterial declarations used by the new grgreens code, including grMaterial_801C8D44 and grMaterial_801C8E48.
Removed local prototype block after declarations were moved to the header.
Split grGr data symbols around strings, tables, grGr_803E7840, and trailing unknown data to match the new static data layout.

what_changed: Expanded the Green Greens stage parameter struct from sparse padding into offset fields through x78, including block timers, block bomb chance, wind timer/speed/bounds fields, and additional unknown numeric fields used by the new logic.

Added static data tables grGr_803E7734, grGr_803E775C, grGr_803E777C, grGr_803E77B4, grGr_803E77D4, grGr_803E780C, and a 30-entry grGr_803E7840 JObj/collision-id table; symbols.txt was updated to split surrounding .data symbols accordingly.

Replaced the commented stub for grGreens_80213C10 with a large state machine using Ground_801C2BA4, grAnime_801C8138/grAnime_801C83D0/grAnime_801C84A4, lbAudioAx_800237A8, Camera_80030E44, lb_80011A50, ftLib_800864A8, HSD_JObjGetTranslation, and it_802EE200. The code drives Green Greens/Whispy animation phases, wind direction state via greens2.x18, and item spawning through the Whispy apple include.
Added struct grGreens_GroundVars2 and changed wind callback fn_80213B1C to read GET_GROUND(ground_gobj)->gv.greens2.x18 instead of overloading the existing greens struct.

Implemented block-grid support functions: grGreens_8021483C computes a 5x6 grid of Vec positions from 30 stage JObjs; grGreens_80214B58 initializes grGr_8049F9E0 block status/type data; grGreens_802150C4 links neighboring collision entries with mpLib_800581DC; fn_80215B84 maps item callbacks back to a block; grGreens_80215D54 shifts blocks after removal; grGreens_80215ED8 updates falling/landing block positions; grGreens_802166C4 handles timed weighted spawning and cleanup; grGreens_80216C20 reconnects links after changes.

Adjusted existing code for matching and correctness: randrange now handles min greater than max, min less than max, and equal values; grGreens_802139C4 uses gobj->user_data and reversed wind timer arguments; grGreens_80214674 uses a temporary Ground_GObj* alias before repeated calls; grGreens_80214FA8 swaps row/column arguments into grGreens_80215358 and uses HSD_ASSERT(1203, NULL); grGreens_80215358 uses clearer col,row parameters and Ground_801C32D4(6, grGr_803E7840[num]).
Added includes for melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h and melee/it/items/itwhispyapple.h to support the newly decompiled audio and item behavior.
smart_moves: Kept unknown stage parameter offsets as xNN fields while naming only the clearly inferred fields such as x0_blockTimerMin, x4_blockTimerMax, x8_blockBombChance, x34_windTimerMin, x38_windTimerMax, and x3C_windSpeed.
Separated Green Greens actor state into greens and greens2 rather than forcing Whispy/wind state into the existing block-grid struct; this made fn_80213B1C's wind flag source explicit.

Recovered static data tables for animation/state sequencing instead of burying the values in switches, and paired that with symbols.txt data splits so future data work can find grGr_803E7734 through grGr_803E787C.
Treated row/column ordering as a first-class matching concern by renaming parameters to col,row in some functions and fixing call sites/loops around getBlock and getVec.

Used source-shape tricks that decompilers should remember: direct gobj->user_data access, an otherwise redundant new_var alias, ternary HSD_Randi calls inside randrange, and NULL rather than 0 in HSD_ASSERT.

Connected the stage implementation to existing engine subsystems instead of local placeholders: grAnime for stage animation, mpLib for collision links, grMaterial for block items/material state, lbAudioAx for SFX, and itwhispyapple for item spawning.
decomp_lessons: For stage files with multiple logical actors, inspect which Ground union member each callback really uses; here, Whispy/wind logic needed a new grGreens_GroundVars2 while block-grid logic stayed in grGreens_GroundVars.

When a stage parameter block begins as padding, progressively replace padding with offset-accurate typed fields as functions consume them. Even partially named offset fields make later state-machine reconstruction easier.
Grid mechanics should establish row/col helper conventions early. This PR shows that a 5-row by 6-column layout affects loops, getBlock/getVec indexing, collision-link updates, and matching.

Data symbol boundaries matter for decomp progress: shrinking grGr_803E7840 from a 128-entry zeroed s16 array to the actual 30-entry table required splitting adjacent data symbols and accepting remaining unknown tails.

Small C expression choices can unlock full matches even when behavior is equivalent. The bot-reported new matches coincided with changes like gobj->user_data access in grGreens_802139C4 and the temporary alias in grGreens_80214674.

Partial implementations can still be valuable: decomp.dev showed major percentage gains for grGreens_80213C10, grGreens_80215ED8, grGreens_802166C4, grGreens_802150C4, fn_80215B84, grGreens_80215D54, and grGreens_80216C20 even though not all reached 100%.
naming_conventions: Stage functions keep the address-based grGreens_802xxxxx naming convention until semantic names are available.
Green Greens file-scope data uses the grGr_ prefix with address-derived suffixes, such as grGr_803E7734, grGr_803E780C, grGr_803E7840, and grGr_8049F9E0.

Offset fields use xNN names, optionally with semantic suffixes when supported by use sites: x0_blockTimerMin, x4_blockTimerMax, x8_blockBombChance, x34_windTimerMin, x38_windTimerMax, x3C_windSpeed, x40_left, x44_right, x48_top, and x4C_bottom.
New alternate Ground union state was named grGreens_GroundVars2 and accessed as gp->gv.greens2.
Block-grid function parameters were clarified as col and row where evidence supported it, while less certain parameters stayed as arg1/arg2 or xNN fields.
Existing block constants retained the Gr_Greens_Block_ prefix style, including Gr_Greens_Block_Status_None and Gr_Greens_Block_Max.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct field access gobj->user_data instead of GET_GROUND(gobj) when register allocation/source shape requires it; this helped grGreens_802139C4 reach 100%.
Preserve apparently redundant aliases such as Ground_GObj* new_var = gobj when they produce the desired call/register pattern; this helped grGreens_80214674 reach 100%.
Keep randrange branch order and ternary HSD_Randi(diff) shape close to assembly, including support for reversed min/max arguments.
Use exact data widths and counts for tables: grGr_803E7840 became s16[30], not a large placeholder array, and nearby symbols were split to represent strings, tables, and unknown trailing data.
Prefer table-driven animation indices for state machines when the .data section exposes those sequences; grGr_803E775C/grGr_803E777C and bg equivalents are indexed directly by state fields.
After mutating the block list during cleanup, the implementation restarts scanning with goto restart_cleanup, matching a likely assembly control-flow pattern and avoiding iterator invalidation.

review_feedback.note: No PR body text, human reviews, or inline review comments were present in the slice, so reviewer intent is not available.
GALE01 matched code increased to 66.22% (+0.01%, +520 bytes); matched data reported 38.70% (-24 bytes).
Two new matches were reported: grGreens_802139C4 improved from 87.57% to 100.00%, and grGreens_80214674 improved from 93.68% to 100.00%.

Large unmatched-item improvements included grGreens_80213C10 to 88.54%, grGreens_80215ED8 to 73.26%, grGreens_802166C4 to 52.72%, fn_80215B84 to 84.79%, grGreens_80215D54 to 70.78%, and grGreens_80216C20 to 60.62%.
Known regressions were called out: .sdata broke from 100.00% to 81.82%, and .data regressed from 27.39% to 25.84%.
